reforms.description: The creation of a National Innovation Council linked to the Presidency aims to advise the government on innovation policy and improve coherence in the innovation system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing a dedicated council is expected to enhance policy coherence and effectiveness in fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The creation of the National Innovation Council linked to the Presidency to advise the government on innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to improve coordination and strategic direction.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of the National Innovation Council linked to the Presidency to advise the government on innovation policy.

reforms.description: The establishment of a National Innovation Council is proposed to coordinate and enhance innovation efforts across sectors in Chile.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The council is expected to improve governance and strategic direction for innovation, facilitating better coordination among stakeholders.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to create a National Innovation Council.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a National Innovation Council signifies a structural change in how innovation is managed and prioritized in Chile, aiming for a more cohesive approach to innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to create a National Innovation Council.

reforms.description: In 2005, the FDI and FONTEC funds were merged into a new programme called Innova Chile, aimed at encouraging business innovation and fostering competitiveness through joint ventures with universities and technological institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The merger aims to streamline public funding for innovation, enhancing collaboration between businesses and research institutions, which can lead to increased R&D activity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The FDI and FONTEC funds were merged into Innova Chile in 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: The merger represents a significant structural change in the public funding landscape for innovation, aiming to improve efficiency and effectiveness in supporting R&D activities.
reforms.source_quote: In 2005, FDI and FONTEC were merged into a new programme: Innova Chile.

reforms.description: A new law to upgrade Chile's intellectual property rights (IPR) legislation is expected to come into force by mid-2005, aiming to enhance legal protections and align with OECD standards.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved IPR protection is likely to encourage innovation by providing stronger legal frameworks for creators and investors, thus fostering a more conducive environment for R&D.
reforms.status_evidence: A new law is set to upgrade Chile’s IPR legislation, expected to enter into force by mid-2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of the legal framework for intellectual property, which is crucial for fostering innovation and protecting creators' rights.
reforms.source_quote: A new law, expected to enter into force by mid-2005, is set to upgrade Chile’s IPR legislation.

reforms.description: The Chilean government plans to double public R&D spending from 0.4% to 0.8% of GDP over the next three years, financed by revenue from a new mining tax, to enhance innovation activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and address market failures in financing for firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to double public R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding landscape for R&D in Chile, aiming to enhance the overall innovation capacity of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government intends to increase public spending on R&D, to be financed by revenue from the mining tax introduced in May 2005.

reforms.description: The Chile Innova programme was launched to consolidate various funds and improve coordination in government support for innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By consolidating funds and improving coordination, the programme aims to enhance the effectiveness of government support for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Recent initiatives to step up coordination include the Chile Innova programme launched by the Ministry of Economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards improving the coherence and effectiveness of innovation support mechanisms in Chile.
reforms.source_quote: Recent initiatives to step up coordination include the Chile Innova programme launched by the Ministry of Economy.

reforms.description: The Chile Innova programme was established to promote the creation of new businesses at the incubator and start-up stages using new or emerging technologies, funding up to CLP35 million per undertaking.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding new businesses, the initiative fosters entrepreneurship and innovation, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has funded 43 projects between 2001 and mid-2003.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to support new business creation, particularly in technology sectors, which is crucial for economic diversification.
reforms.source_quote: This programme promotes the creation of new businesses at the incubator and start-up stages using new or emerging technologies.

reforms.description: The new IPR law was approved by Congress in March 2005, aimed at enhancing intellectual property rights to support innovation and technology transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening intellectual property rights encourages innovation by providing legal protections for new inventions and technologies, thus incentivizing R&D investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The new IPR law was approved by Congress in March 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in the legal framework governing intellectual property, which is essential for fostering innovation and attracting investment.
reforms.source_quote: The new IPR law was approved by Congress in March 2005, but will not enter into force until issuance of specific regulations.

reforms.description: The Chilean government approved changes to the electricity legislation in May 2005 to remove obstacles to investment in generation, addressing supply insecurity and reducing the likelihood of energy shortages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating investment in energy generation, the reform aims to enhance energy supply security, which is crucial for economic growth and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Changes to the legislation were approved by Congress in May 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the energy sector, aimed at enhancing investment and ensuring energy security, which is vital for the overall economy.
reforms.source_quote: It is expected that changes to the legislation, approved by Congress in May 2005 (Ley Corta II), will remove the obstacles to investment in generation associated with the insecurity of supply.

reforms.description: The creation of the National Innovation Council aims to enhance policy coordination and improve the allocation of government funds towards business-financed innovation, aligning with Chile's comparative advantages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving policy coordination and funding allocation is expected to stimulate business innovation, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The creation of the National Innovation Council is mentioned as a means to boost policy coordination.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a National Innovation Council represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the innovation ecosystem in Chile, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of the National Innovation Council can do much to boost policy coordination but the allocation of government funds will need to be carried out in a cost-effective, transparent, contestable fashion.

reforms.description: The Chilean government plans to allocate revenue from a newly introduced mining tax to a fund for innovation, aiming to enhance the country's National Innovation System through better policy coordination and long-term planning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allocation of mining tax revenues to innovation funding is expected to stimulate R&D investment and improve innovation performance in the country.
reforms.status_evidence: The mining tax was introduced and revenues are being allocated to the innovation fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in funding for innovation, leveraging natural resource revenues to support R&D and innovation initiatives.
reforms.source_quote: Revenues will be allocated to a fund for innovation... with 15% of disbursements assigned to the mining regions and 10% for the other regions.

reforms.description: The new IPR law will align Chilean IPR legislation with WTO's TRIPs Agreement, enhancing protection for trade secrets and pharmaceuticals, and improving enforcement mechanisms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening IPR protection is expected to incentivize innovation and patenting, particularly in industries reliant on intellectual property.
reforms.status_evidence: The law's main provisions have been outlined and are pending implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of IPR legislation, which is crucial for fostering innovation and aligning with international standards.
reforms.source_quote: The new IPR law will bring Chilean IPR legislation into line with the requirements of the WTO’s Trade Related Intellectual Property Rights Agreement (TRIPs).
